questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the differential equation $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}(\mathrm{ydx}-\mathrm{dy})=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{x}}(\mathrm{ydx}+\mathrm{dy})$. Let $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ be a particular solution to this differential equation which passes through the point $(0,2)$ Let $C \equiv y=\log _{\frac{1}{4}}\left(x-\frac{1}{4}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \log _4\left(16 x^2-8 x+1\right)$, be another curve. On the basis of above information, answer the following questions :Question: The area bounded by the curve C, parabola $x=y^2+\frac{1}{4}$ and the line $x=\frac{1}{4}$ is -
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the differential equation $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}(\mathrm{ydx}-\mathrm{dy})=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{x}}(\mathrm{ydx}+\mathrm{dy})$. Let $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ be a particular solution to this differential equation which passes through the point $(0,2)$ Let $C \equiv y=\log _{\frac{1}{4}}\left(x-\frac{1}{4}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \log _4\left(16 x^2-8 x+1\right)$, be another curve. On the basis of above information, answer the following questions :Question: If the area bounded by the curve $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$, curve C , ordinate $\mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{4}$ \& the ordinate $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{a}$ is $4-\ell \operatorname{nn} 4+\frac{1}{\sqrt[4]{\mathrm{e}}}-\sqrt[4]{\mathrm{e}}$, then the value of a is -
